items.label: Enterprise

transforms.code:
# Define database name (replace with your actual database name)
    dbName = "mes_core"
    
    # Define the query with hardcoded ParentID (PostgreSQL with column aliases)
    query = """SELECT 
        id AS \"ID\",
        parentid AS \"ParentID\",
        reasonname AS \"ReasonName\", 
        reasoncode AS \"ReasonCode\",
        recorddowntime AS \"RecordDowntime\",
        planneddowntime AS \"PlannedDowntime\",
        operatorselectable AS \"OperatorSelectable\",
        subreasonof AS \"SubReasonOf\"
    FROM statereason WHERE parentid = ?"""
    try:
        # Execute the query with ParentID = 1
        data = system.db.runPrepQuery(query, [1], dbName)
        
        # Convert dataset to JSON for Perspective compatibility
        json_data = system.dataset.toPyDataSet(data)
        result = [
            {
                col: row[col]
                for col in data.getColumnNames()
            }
            for row in json_data
        ]
        return result
        
        # Uncomment the following line to return the dataset directly (e.g., for Vision)
        # return data
        
    except Exception as e:
        system.util.getLogger("ScriptLogger").error("Query failed: {}".format(str(e)))
        return []

    dbName = "mes_core"
    
    # Define the query with hardcoded ParentID (PostgreSQL with column aliases)
    query = """SELECT 
        id AS \"ID\",
        tagpath AS \"TagPath\",
        parentid AS \"ParentID\"
    FROM counttag WHERE parentid = ?"""
    try:

# Define database name (replace with your actual database name)
    dbName = "mes_core"
    
    # Define the query (PostgreSQL with column aliases)
    query = """SELECT 
        id AS \"ID\",
        counttype AS \"CountType\"
    FROM counttype"""
    try:
        # Execute the query
        data = system.db.runQuery(query, dbName)
        
        # Convert dataset to JSON for Perspective compatibility
        json_data = system.dataset.toPyDataSet(data)
        result = [
            {
                col: row[col]
                for col in data.getColumnNames()
            }
            for row in json_data
        ]
        return result
        
        # Uncomment the following line to return the dataset directly (e.g., for Vision)
        # return data
        
    except Exception as e:
        system.util.getLogger("ScriptLogger").error("Query failed: {}".format(str(e)))
        return []
